root.description: The name of the maintenance track for the restored cluster. When you take a snapshot, the snapshot inherits the MaintenanceTrack value from the cluster. The snapshot might be on a different track than the cluster that was the source for the snapshot. Default value is `current`
root.name: maintenance_track_name

root.description: If true, the cluster can be accessed from a public network
root.name: publicly_accessible

root.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the IAM role that was set as default for the cluster when the cluster was created
root.name: default_iam_role_arn

root.description: Map of authentication profiles to create
root.name: authentication_profiles

root.description: Determines whether a CloudWatch log group is created for each `var.logging.log_exports`
root.name: create_cloudwatch_log_group

root.description: The default number of days to retain a manual snapshot. If the value is -1, the snapshot is retained indefinitely. This setting doesn't change the retention period of existing snapshots. Valid values are between `-1` and `3653`. Default value is `-1`
root.name: manual_snapshot_retention_period

root.description: ID of the KMS key used to encrypt the cluster admin credentials secret
root.name: master_password_secret_kms_key_id

root.description: Username for the master DB user. Defaults to `awsuser`
root.name: master_username

root.description: Determines whether a final snapshot of the cluster is created before Redshift deletes the cluster. If true, a final cluster snapshot is not created. If false , a final cluster snapshot is created before the cluster is deleted
root.name: skip_final_snapshot

root.description: The name of the snapshot from which to create the new cluster. Conflicts with `snapshot_arn`
root.name: snapshot_identifier

root.description: Map of scheduled action definitions to create
root.name: scheduled_actions

root.description: The number of days to retain CloudWatch logs for the redshift cluster
root.name: cloudwatch_log_group_retention_in_days

root.description: Determines whether to create security group for Redshift cluster
root.name: create_security_group

root.description: The EC2 Availability Zone (AZ) in which you want Amazon Redshift to provision the cluster. Can only be changed if `availability_zone_relocation_enabled` is `true`
root.name: availability_zone

root.description: If `true`, the cluster can be relocated to another availability zone, either automatically by AWS or when requested. Default is `false`. Available for use on clusters from the RA3 instance family
root.name: availability_zone_relocation_enabled

root.description: The ARN for the KMS encryption key. When specifying `kms_key_arn`, `encrypted` needs to be set to `true`
root.name: kms_key_arn

root.description: Whether to use AWS SecretsManager to manage the cluster admin credentials. Conflicts with `master_password_wo`. One of `master_password_wo` or `manage_master_password` is required unless `snapshot_identifier` is provided
root.name: manage_master_password

root.description: Determines whether to create a parameter group or use existing
root.name: create_parameter_group

root.description: Specifies whether to rotate the secret immediately or wait until the next scheduled rotation window
root.name: master_password_rotate_immediately

root.description: Map of security group egress rules to add to the security group created
root.name: security_group_egress_rules
root.type:
map(object({
    name = optional(string)

    cidr_ipv4                    = optional(string)
    cidr_ipv6                    = optional(string)
    description                  = optional(string)
    from_port                    = optional(number)
    ip_protocol                  = optional(string, "tcp")
    prefix_list_id               = optional(string)
    referenced_security_group_id = optional(string)
    region                       = optional(string)
    tags                         = optional(map(string), {})
    to_port                      = optional(number)
  }))

root.description: The name of the cluster the source snapshot was created from
root.name: snapshot_cluster_identifier

root.description: Create, update, and delete timeout configurations for the cluster
root.name: cluster_timeouts

root.description: ARN of the policy that is used to set the permissions boundary for the scheduled action IAM role
root.name: iam_role_permissions_boundary

root.description: The security group name
root.name: security_group_name

root.description: Determines whether to create Redshift cluster and resources (affects all resources)
root.name: create

root.description: The Cluster Identifier. Must be a lower case string
root.name: cluster_identifier

root.description: The name of the Redshift subnet group, existing or to be created
root.name: subnet_group_name

root.description: Additional tags to add to the subnet group
root.name: subnet_group_tags

root.description: Map of usage limit definitions to create
root.name: usage_limits
root.type:
map(object({
    amount        = number
    breach_action = optional(string)
    feature_type  = string
    limit_type    = optional(string) # Will fall back to key if not set
    period        = optional(string)
    tags          = optional(map(string), {})
  }))

root.description: Specifies whether any cluster modifications are applied immediately, or during the next maintenance window. Default is `false`
root.name: apply_immediately

root.description: The family of the Redshift parameter group
root.name: parameter_group_family

root.description: Determines whether the security group name (`security_group_name`) is used as a prefix
root.name: security_group_use_name_prefix

root.description: Number of nodes in the cluster. Defaults to 1. Note: values greater than 1 will trigger `cluster_type` to switch to `multi-node`
root.name: number_of_nodes

root.description: A list of Redshift parameters to apply
root.name: parameter_group_parameters

root.description: Additional tags to add to cloudwatch log groups created
root.name: cloudwatch_log_group_tags

root.description: Configuration of automatic copy of snapshots from one region to another
root.name: snapshot_copy

root.description: Map of security group ingress rules to add to the security group created
root.name: security_group_ingress_rules
root.type:
map(object({
    name = optional(string)

    cidr_ipv4                    = optional(string)
    cidr_ipv6                    = optional(string)
    description                  = optional(string)
    from_port                    = optional(number)
    ip_protocol                  = optional(string, "tcp")
    prefix_list_id               = optional(string)
    referenced_security_group_id = optional(string)
    region                       = optional(string)
    tags                         = optional(map(string), {})
    to_port                      = optional(number)
  }))

root.description: The description of the security group. If value is set to empty string it will contain cluster name in the description
root.name: security_group_description

root.description: If `true`, the data in the cluster is encrypted at rest
root.name: encrypted

root.description: The description of the Redshift Subnet group. Defaults to `Managed by Terraform`
root.name: subnet_group_description

root.description: Determines whether a scheduled action IAM role is created
root.name: create_scheduled_action_iam_role

root.description: Description of the scheduled action IAM role
root.name: iam_role_description

root.description: Whether to manage the master user password rotation. Setting this value to false after previously having been set to true will disable automatic rotation
root.name: manage_master_password_rotation

root.description: The length of the rotation window in hours. For example, 3h for a three hour window
root.name: master_password_rotation_duration

root.description: A cron() or rate() expression that defines the schedule for rotating your secret. Either `master_user_password_rotation_automatically_after_days` or `master_user_password_rotation_schedule_expression` must be specified
root.name: master_password_rotation_schedule_expression

root.description: An array of VPC subnet IDs to use in the subnet group
root.name: subnet_ids

root.description: Region where the resource(s) will be managed. Defaults to the Region set in the provider configuration
root.name: region

root.description: If `true`, enhanced VPC routing is enabled
root.name: enhanced_vpc_routing

root.description: The node type to be provisioned for the cluster
root.name: node_type

root.description: A list of Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) security groups to be associated with the cluster
root.name: vpc_security_group_ids

root.description: Determines whether to create a subnet group or use existing
root.name: create_subnet_group

root.description: Determines whether scheduled action the IAM role name (`iam_role_name`) is used as a prefix
root.name: iam_role_use_name_prefix

root.description: A map of additional tags to add to the scheduled action IAM role created
root.name: iam_role_tags

root.description: The ARN of the KMS Key to use when encrypting log data
root.name: cloudwatch_log_group_kms_key_id

root.description: If `true`, major version upgrades can be applied during the maintenance window to the Amazon Redshift engine that is running on the cluster. Default is `true`
root.name: allow_version_upgrade

root.description: The identifier of the final snapshot that is to be created immediately before deleting the cluster. If this parameter is provided, `skip_final_snapshot` must be `false`
root.name: final_snapshot_identifier

root.description: The name of the Redshift parameter group, existing or to be created
root.name: parameter_group_name

root.description: The description of the Redshift parameter group. Defaults to `Managed by Terraform`
root.name: parameter_group_description

root.description: Additional tags for the security group
root.name: security_group_tags

root.description: A map of tags to add to all resources
root.name: tags

root.description: The name of the first database to be created when the cluster is created. If you do not provide a name, Amazon Redshift will create a default database called `dev`
root.name: database_name

root.description: Password for the master DB user. Must contain at least 8 chars, one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, and one number
root.name: master_password_wo

root.description: The AWS customer account used to create or copy the snapshot. Required if you are restoring a snapshot you do not own, optional if you own the snapshot
root.name: owner_account

root.description: The weekly time range (in UTC) during which automated cluster maintenance can occur. Format: `ddd:hh24:mi-ddd:hh24:mi`
root.name: preferred_maintenance_window

root.description: A list of IAM Role ARNs to associate with the cluster. A Maximum of 10 can be associated to the cluster at any time
root.name: iam_role_arns

root.description: ID of the VPC where to create security group
root.name: vpc_id

root.description: Used together with `master_password_wo` to trigger an update. Increment this value when an update to the `master_password_wo` is required
root.name: master_password_wo_version

root.description: Specifies if the Redshift cluster is multi-AZ
root.name: multi_az

root.description: The port number on which the cluster accepts incoming connections. Default port is `5439`
root.name: port

root.description: Additional tags to add to the parameter group
root.name: parameter_group_tags

root.description: Name to use on scheduled action IAM role created
root.name: iam_role_name

root.description: Scheduled action IAM role path
root.name: iam_role_path

root.description: Logging configuration for the cluster
root.name: logging

root.description: Set to true if you do not wish the log group (and any logs it may contain) to be deleted at destroy time, and instead just remove the log group from the Terraform state
root.name: cloudwatch_log_group_skip_destroy

root.description: The ARN of the snapshot from which to create the new cluster. Conflicts with `snapshot_identifier`
root.name: snapshot_arn

root.description: Configuration for creating a snapshot schedule and associating it with the cluster
root.name: snapshot_schedule
root.type:
object({
    definitions   = list(string)
    description   = optional(string)
    force_destroy = optional(bool)
    use_prefix    = optional(bool, false)
    identifier    = optional(string)
    tags          = optional(map(string), {})
  })

root.description: Map of endpoint access (managed VPC endpoint) definitions to create
root.name: endpoint_access

root.description: Specifies the number of days between automatic scheduled rotations of the secret. Either `master_user_password_rotation_automatically_after_days` or `master_user_password_rotation_schedule_expression` must be specified
root.name: master_password_rotation_automatically_after_days

root.description: The number of days that automated snapshots are retained. If the value is 0, automated snapshots are disabled. Even if automated snapshots are disabled, you can still create manual snapshots when you want with create-cluster-snapshot. Default is 1
root.name: automated_snapshot_retention_period

root.description: The version of the Amazon Redshift engine software that you want to deploy on the cluster. The version selected runs on all the nodes in the cluster
root.name: cluster_version

root.description: The Elastic IP (EIP) address for the cluster
root.name: elastic_ip

root.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Redshift subnet group created
root.name: subnet_group_arn

root.description: The name of the parameter group to be associated with this cluster
root.name: cluster_parameter_group_name

root.description: The Redshift cluster ID
root.name: cluster_id

root.description: A map of maps containing scheduled action details
root.name: scheduled_actions

root.description: Map of authentication profiles created and their associated attributes
root.name: authentication_profiles

root.description: Specifies whether automatic rotation is enabled for the secret
root.name: cluster_secretsmanager_secret_rotation_enabled

root.description: The VPC security group ids associated with the cluster
root.name: cluster_vpc_security_group_ids

root.description: The specific revision number of the database in the cluster
root.name: cluster_revision_number

root.description: Map of usage limits created and their associated attributes
root.name: usage_limits

root.description: The availability zone of the Cluster
root.name: cluster_availability_zone

root.description: The backup retention period
root.name: cluster_automated_snapshot_retention_period

root.description: The hostname of the Redshift cluster
root.name: cluster_hostname

root.description: The port the cluster responds on
root.name: cluster_port

root.description: Stable and unique string identifying the scheduled action IAM role
root.name: scheduled_action_iam_role_unique_id

root.description: The name of the Redshift parameter group created
root.name: parameter_group_id

root.description: The name of the default database in the Cluster
root.name: cluster_database_name

root.description: The backup window
root.name: cluster_preferred_maintenance_window

root.description: The connection endpoint
root.name: cluster_endpoint

root.description: Whether the data in the cluster is encrypted
root.name: cluster_encrypted

root.description: The version of Redshift engine software
root.name: cluster_version

root.description: The nodes in the cluster. Each node is a map of the following attributes: `node_role`, `private_ip_address`, and `public_ip_address`
root.name: cluster_nodes

root.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the parameter group created
root.name: parameter_group_arn

root.description: The Redshift cluster type
root.name: cluster_type

root.description: The type of nodes in the cluster
root.name: cluster_node_type

root.description: The DNS name of the cluster
root.name: cluster_dns_name

root.description: The public key for the cluster
root.name: cluster_public_key

root.description: Scheduled actions IAM role ARN
root.name: scheduled_action_iam_role_arn

root.description: The Redshift cluster ARN
root.name: cluster_arn

root.description: The Redshift cluster identifier
root.name: cluster_identifier

root.description: The name of a cluster subnet group to be associated with this cluster
root.name: cluster_subnet_group_name

root.description: The ID of Redshift Subnet group created
root.name: subnet_group_id

root.description: A map of access endpoints created and their attributes
root.name: endpoint_access

root.description: ARN of managed master password secret
root.name: master_password_secret_arn

root.description: The namespace Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the cluster
root.name: cluster_namespace_arn

root.description: The Redshift cluster master password
root.name: cluster_master_password

root.description: The Redshift cluster master username
root.name: cluster_master_username

root.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Redshift Snapshot Schedule
root.name: snapshot_schedule_arn

root.description: Scheduled actions IAM role name
root.name: scheduled_action_iam_role_name
